items.headline: Crypto’s security nightmare won’t be solved by ordinary audits
items.subheadline: Without an update to the current auditing infrastructure, the crypto space will likely continue to suffer significant losses, explains Beyer.
items.author: Stefan Beyer

items.headline: The SEC delayed tokenizing stocks, and here’s why that’s a relief
items.subheadline: Before we rush to tokenize U.S. equities, we need compliance systems that understand context, not just checkboxes. Investors like Michael Burry see the danger, so should the rest of us, urges Chamarajnagar.
items.author: Dr. Ravishankar Chamarajnagar

items.headline: The banking lobby is wrong about stablecoins and community banks
items.subheadline: Congress should not kneecap one of the clearest advances in payment infrastructure to protect community banks from a threat that has not been proven, argues Eco CEO Ryne Saxe.
items.author: Ryne Sax

items.headline: What Robinhood’s recent layoffs say about the current state of crypto investments
items.subheadline: Despite the trading platform’s restructuring and the wave of crypto companies making cuts, signaling a late bear market, Altcoin Pro’s Horst, Anderson and Zhuleku explain there’s no reason to panic.
items.author: Ryan Horst

items.headline: BlackRock (BLK) crypto push deepens with Ethena integration, sending ENA up 8%
items.subheadline: The deal adds Ethena's yield-generating token to BlackRock's risk management platform and create a $100 million liquidity facility for BlackRock's tokenized money market fund.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Ukraine transfers $8.3 million in seized crypto amid potential plans for strategic reserve
items.subheadline: The funds were seized during an investigation into an international hacker group accused of laundering proceeds from data theft through high-value property.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Strategy authorizes bitcoin sales under new monetization framework
items.subheadline: The company can now sell bitcoin to fund the USD reserve, support preferred dividends, and finance up to $2 billion in stock buybacks.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Wall Street's BNY expands stablecoin ties with Circle, lets institutions mint and hold USDC
items.subheadline: The world's largest custody bank is adding USDC custody and minting services as stablecoins move deeper into traditional finance.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Ripple news: XRP Ledger moves to add onchain lending in latest moves
items.subheadline: A proposed XRPL standard would let institutions borrow against tokenized assets, with the blockchain enforcing loan terms while the underwriting stays with human credit teams. It still needs validator approval to go live.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Post-prison CZ says time behind bars didn't hurt the billionaire's business after Binance
items.subheadline: The pardoned ex-CEO called prison a character-building "plus" as he's pursued a venture-capital career, despite his majority stake in the world's biggest crypto exchange.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: How to better understand bitcoin’s perpetual identity crisis
items.subheadline: Here, Bailey unpacks the uncertainty surrounding the original cryptocurrency’s nature, which creates inconsistent market behavior.
items.author: Steve Bailey

items.headline: Quantum computing is often seen as a risk to bitcoin. Now Trump wants to develop it.
items.subheadline: The White House issued twin executive orders to accelerate U.S. development of large-scale quantum computers while simultaneously hardening defenses against such machines.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: BIS says stablecoins are more like ETFs than actual money
items.subheadline: BIS's latest annual report dives into stablecoins and AI trends.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Crypto exchange BitMEX loses CEO, CFO and head of growth
items.subheadline: The firm’s global general counsel, Peter Wilkinson, has taken over as CEO from Stephan Lutz.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: FundBank rebrands as IRACE, buys Cayman-based Tenet to expand digital asset services
items.subheadline: The institutional banking provider is expanding into digital asset custody, liquidity and execution services as former Zodia Custody Ireland CEO John Cronin takes the helm.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Crypto perpetuals unlikely to displace legacy futures markets, JPMorgan says
items.subheadline: The bank said institutional demand for perpetual futures remains muted, with the products better suited to speculative trading than hedging.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Tom Lee blames bitcoin, ether weakness on quarter-end rebalancing as Bitmine (BMNR) buys $43M ETH
items.subheadline: Bitmine made its smallest purchase since early May as Lee pointed to investors cutting losses before the start of the second half of the year.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Prediction market consolidation could spark wave of M&A across sports betting, Bernstein says
items.subheadline: The broker said operational consolidation is blurring the lines between exchanges, brokerages and sportsbooks, creating conditions for acquisitions across the prediction market ecosystem.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: New MiCA regime in Europe, U.S. nonfarm payrolls for June: Crypto Week Ahead: Crypto Week Ahead
items.subheadline: Your look at what's coming in the week starting June 29.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Bitcoin remains below key onchain and technical levels, leaving it in no man's land
items.subheadline: Bitcoin is trading below key technical and onchain valuation levels, with historical bear market patterns suggesting a potential bottom closer to $45,000.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Bitcoin (BTC) price steadies as analysts warn more downside lies ahead
items.subheadline: BTC edged up 0.6% to $59,800 to start the week while SOL rose 2%, though derivatives data and chart formations point to continued downside risk.
items.author: Oliver Knight

items.headline: USD-INR price: USDT jumps to 8.5% premium in India after crypto payment crackdown
items.subheadline: Raids on crypto payment firms in Bengaluru disrupted the pipeline that feeds dollar-pegged USDT to Indian platforms, pushing its local price more than 8.5% above the dollar, roughly double the usual gap.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Strategy announces $2 billion buybacks, bitcoin monetization plan and new capital framework
items.subheadline: Strategy adopted a new capital management framework, authorizing up to $2 billion in buybacks while creating a program that allows future bitcoin sales to support liquidity.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: Foundation cuts 20% of staff amid leadership exodus
items.subheadline: The reduction follows a period of significant upheaval at the organization’s leadership level.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Vitalik Buterin says Ethereum Foundation will cut budget 40% in major reset
items.subheadline: The reduction comes on the same day the EF confirmed a 20% reduction in headcount and follows the resignation of co-Executive Director Hsiao-Wei Wang. Her departure brings the total number of senior Ethereum Foundation figures to leave since January to nine, underscoring the scale of the organization's ongoing turmoil.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin claws back some of the losses after Micron beats earnings
items.subheadline: Battered crypto sector gets some relief after Micron beats earnings, bringing back some risk appetite to the market.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: Upheaval at the foundation has some of crypto’s biggest names feeling bullish
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we're looking at Ethereum's eventful week that started off with the launch of EthLabs, plus the layoffs at the Ethereum Foundation, and what this all means for the network.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Live markets: Bitcoin, ether lead $1 billion liquidation losses as AI trade keeps going
items.subheadline: Strategy's common stock hit a multi-year low, while its STRC preferred stock plunged to a new record low.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Coinbase (COIN) news: Base blockchain resumes after two-hour outage disrupted network
items.subheadline: The incident temporarily halted transaction processing on one of Ethereum's largest layer-2 networks.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Live markets: Bitcoin falls below $60,000. Kospi, Nikkei sink
items.subheadline: BTC sees a relief bounce as Asian stocks wilt following sharp losses on Wall Street.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Live BTC markets: Bitcoin above $60,000 as Strategy rolls out BTC monetization plan
items.subheadline: Stocks are about to close out their best quarter since 2020 as crypto continues to get crushed.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Vitalik Buterin says crypto’s most powerful idea is still nowhere near ready
items.subheadline: The Ethereum co-founder says indistinguishability obfuscation could one day act like a “trustless trusted third party,” but today’s versions remain far too slow for real use.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Why tokenization is an ETF-style market structure revolution
items.subheadline: The current tokenization dialogue and pattern resemble ETFs’ early days, which ultimately transformed into a $10+ trillion market, Lie argues.
items.author: Michael Lie

items.headline: Meta is paying creators in Stablecoins. Spending them is someone else's problem
items.subheadline: Meta’s decision to pay creators in USDC validates stablecoins as a mainstream disbursement tool, Joslyn suggests, but it also exposes the industry’s unresolved problem: moving seamlessly from digital dollars to usable local currency.
items.author: Tim Joslyn

items.headline: 5 corruption gaps Congress must close in the Clarity Act
items.subheadline: The most consequential crypto legislation in the world is moving forward in the U.S. Senate. As written, it leaves the United States exposed to money laundering, sanctions evasion, and conflicts of interest at the highest levels of government, argues Greytak.
items.author: Scott Greytak

items.headline: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
items.subheadline: A recent research note published by Citi analysts reached a conclusion about quantum risk that should give every institutional bitcoin holder pause, Tabar explains.
items.author: Samir Tabar

items.headline: The U.S. government is betting $2 Billion on quantum computing, and the defense side can't keep up
items.subheadline: Pruden argues that to defend against a quantum computer capable of cryptographically relevant operations, we need post-quantum cryptography and regulatory coordination that the industry has been deferring for years.
items.author: Alex  Pruden

items.headline: Stablecoins Were Meant to Disrupt Finance. Instead, They Became Idle Cash.
items.subheadline: O’Connor argues that crypto’s clearest success story has scaled as money but not as capital.
items.author: John O’Connor

items.headline: If America wants to lead in crypto, it must protect the people who build it
items.subheadline: Despite the Clarity Act’s advancement toward the finish line, there’s one provision under threat for builders that can’t be overlooked, argues Smith.
items.author: Kristin Smith

items.headline: Kraken sues crypto derivatives firm PowerTrade over missing funds
items.subheadline: PowerTrade changed Kraken’s positive balance of over $6 milllion to a $2 million deficit via a series of unauthorized “corrections” related to trades that had expired or settled months earlier, according to a legal filing.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: Binance tells EU users it will no longer provide services after failing to secure MiCA license
items.subheadline: A day after withdrawing its MiCA license application in Greece and saying it is ‘not leaving Europe,’ Binance notified users across the European Union that it will suspend services.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Anti-trafficking group says CLARITY Act's Section 604 could weaken accountability
items.subheadline: An anti-human trafficking advocate said a Clarity Act provision could weaken accountability despite existing criminal laws.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: U.S. House Democrat, who may soon run key committee, condemns crypto in 401(k)s
items.subheadline: Maxine Waters, the ranking Democrat on House Financial Services, asked the chief of the Department of Labor to withdraw its proposal on alternative assets.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Binance founder CZ blames crypto's sour 2026 on mix of AI, global tension, 4-year cycle
items.subheadline: There is no single cause for the crypto market's 50% decline over the past year, the Binance founder told CoinDesk.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: CZ wants to make the U.S. the 'capital of crypto': State of Crypto
items.subheadline: The Binance founder shared his views on a variety of topics in an exclusive interview with CoinDesk earlier this month.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Europe’s unlicensed crypto firms face ‘wipeout’ as MiCA transition deadline nears
items.subheadline: European regulator ESMA called on unauthorized crypto-asset service providers to wind down their businesses in an orderly manner as the MiCA transitional period ends on July 1.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: Millions of European crypto users face a sudden hunt for new digital asset platforms
items.subheadline: New MiCA regulations are forcing unlicensed firms to wind down, leaving up to 80% of Europe's current crypto platforms at risk of shutting down.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Crypto analytics firm Chainalysis proposes standards for blockchain tracing
items.subheadline: The blockchain analysis company published a proposed ontology for how investigators can identify clusters of crypto addresses and link them together.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: J.P. Morgan broadens Kinexys blockchain settlement network as banks modernize cross-border payments
items.subheadline: The bank added five Asia-Pacific currencies to its Kinexys blockchain platform, allowing institutional clients to settle payments and FX around the clock.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: White House to speak with law enforcement groups to push Crypto's Clarity Act
items.subheadline: Officials from the White House are said to be sitting down with law enforcement representatives who have objected to illicit-finance portions of the bill.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Private keys, not smart contracts, caused 40% of crypto's $16 billion hack losses. Here's whats being done.
items.subheadline: The industry is moving toward fixing the private key vulnerability issue, just not evenly, Wish Wu, co-founder and CEO of Pharos, said.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: JPMorgan urges strong safeguards as congress weighs crypto market structure rules
items.subheadline: The bank says Congress should pair regulatory clarity with safeguards as Senate negotiates the Clarity Act.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Bitcoin-backed lending is making a comeback, according to Silicon Valley Bank
items.subheadline: The bank said bitcoin lending has emerged from the 2022 crypto credit collapse with stronger risk controls, growing institutional participation and a path toward lower borrowing costs.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Securitize heads to NYSE debut after investors approve SPAC merger;  CEPT gains 20%
items.subheadline: The tokenization firm is expected to begin trading on the NYSE on Thursday, becoming one of the first publicly traded pure-play tokenization companies.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Bitcoin (BTC) news: Saylor kicks the can down the road and yen hits 40-year low. what next?
items.subheadline: BTC fell in Asia as the Japanese yen tanked to four-decade lows, lifting the dollar higher across the board.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: USDT trades at 7%-10% premium in India. Exchanges say it's just supply and demand
items.subheadline: Executives at major platforms CoinDCX and CoinSwitch say the premium reflects a demand-supply imbalance and thin local liquidity.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Ether, doge price news: Cryptos slide as Strategy's bitcoin sales plan pressures market
items.subheadline: Bitcoin held below $60,000 as a surging dollar kept crypto pinned. Onchain demand stayed quiet through the week's losses, and Strategy's plan to possibly sell bitcoin added to the caution.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: XRP price news: What next as Ripple-linked token holds $1 support
items.subheadline: Active addresses jumped 72% in two weeks while open interest fell to its lowest level since July 2025, giving traders a cleaner setup even as price remains trapped below resistance.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Why crypto founders are ditching Europe for Dubai ahead of a major deadline
items.subheadline: While major players like Binance scramble to adjust to the strict new rulebook, smaller startups are fleeing to regions with faster, crypto-specific licensing.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Live markets: BlackRock's IBIT sheds $300 million as bitcoin demand dwindles
items.subheadline: IBIT alone shed $300 million while smaller funds absorbed some of the outflow. The selloff lands as the same AI trade that crashed Korea a week ago now powers a record quarterly rally there.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bitcoin's tie to USD/JPY is the strongest it's been since 2022. Here's why that matters.
items.subheadline: Bitcoin’s price has shown an unusually strong negative 52-week correlation with the dollar-yen exchange rate. Here's what it means.
items.author: Omkar Godbole
